dialogue.t: —Je te lâche jamais vraiment, tu sais. T'es toujours avec nous, ici.
dialogue.en: I never really let go of you, you know. You're always with us, here.
dialogue.lit: I you release never really, you know. You-are always with us, here.

dialogue.t: —Votre vol va bientôt embarquer, monsieur. Vous devez y aller.
dialogue.en: Your flight is about to board, sir. You need to go.
dialogue.lit: Your flight goes soon to-board, sir. You must there to-go.

dialogue.t: —J'arrive ! Un dernier câlin, à tous les deux, s'il vous plaît.
dialogue.en: I'm coming! One last hug, both of you, please.
dialogue.lit: I-arrive! A last hug, to all the two, if it you pleases.

dialogue.t: —On t'aime, Hugo. Reviens vite, promis ?
dialogue.en: We love you, Hugo. Come back soon, promise?
dialogue.lit: One you loves, Hugo. Come-back quickly, promised?

dialogue.t: —Promis, juré. À très bientôt, mes amis. Vraiment, à très bientôt.
dialogue.en: Promise, swear. See you very soon, my friends. Really, very soon.
dialogue.lit: Promised, sworn. To very soon, my friends. Really, to very soon.

dialogue.t: —J'ai décidé de revenir, finalement. Pas pour toujours, mais pour de bon, un peu.
dialogue.en: I decided to come back, after all. Not forever, but for real, a bit.
dialogue.lit: I-have decided to come-back, finally. Not for always, but for of good, a little.

dialogue.t: —Quoi ?! Hugo, tu reviens vivre ici ?!
dialogue.en: What?! Hugo, you're coming back to live here?!
dialogue.lit: What?! Hugo, you come-back to-live here?!

dialogue.t: —Pas exactement. J'ai trouvé un poste qui me permet de travailler d'ici aussi.
dialogue.en: Not exactly. I found a position that lets me work from here too.
dialogue.lit: Not exactly. I-have found a position that me permits to-work from-here also.

dialogue.t: —C'est incroyable ! Marco va halluciner quand il va l'apprendre.
dialogue.en: That's incredible! Marco's going to flip out when he hears.
dialogue.lit: It-is incredible! Marco goes to-hallucinate when he goes it to-learn.

dialogue.t: —Je marche dans ces rues, et tout est tellement familier, inchangé presque.
dialogue.en: I'm walking these streets, and everything's so familiar, almost unchanged.
dialogue.lit: I walk in these streets, and all is so familiar, unchanged almost.

dialogue.t: —Tu te sens nostalgique ?
dialogue.en: Are you feeling nostalgic?
dialogue.lit: You yourself feel nostalgic?

dialogue.t: —Énormément. Chaque coin de rue me rappelle un souvenir avec vous.
dialogue.en: Enormously. Every street corner reminds me of a memory with you.
dialogue.lit: Enormously. Each corner of street me reminds a memory with you.

dialogue.t: —Le café est toujours là, regarde. Rien n'a changé, vraiment.
dialogue.en: The café's still there, look. Nothing's changed, really.
dialogue.lit: The café is always there, look. Nothing not-has changed, really.

dialogue.t: —Je me sens chez moi, ici, comme avant. C'est un sentiment incroyable.
dialogue.en: I feel at home here, like before. It's an incredible feeling.
dialogue.lit: I myself feel at me, here, like before. It-is a feeling incredible.

dialogue.t: —Bienvenue chez toi, Hugo. On t'a gardé une place, toujours.
dialogue.en: Welcome home, Hugo. We always kept a place for you.
dialogue.lit: Welcome at you, Hugo. One you has kept a place, always.

dialogue.t: —Regardez-nous tous, autour de cette table, comme avant.
dialogue.en: Look at all of us, around this table, like before.
dialogue.lit: Look-us all, around this table, like before.

dialogue.t: —Sauf que maintenant, il y a un bébé qui dort dans son siège là-bas.
dialogue.en: Except now there's a baby sleeping in his seat over there.
dialogue.lit: Except that now, it there has a baby that sleeps in his seat there-down.

dialogue.t: —Et un stand de crêpes qui marche super bien, et un poste à Lyon ET ici.
dialogue.en: And a crêpe stand that's doing great, and a job in Lyon AND here.
dialogue.lit: And a stand of crêpes that works super well, and a position at Lyon AND here.

dialogue.t: —Quand je pense au chemin parcouru depuis ce premier café ensemble...
dialogue.en: When I think of the road we've traveled since that first coffee together...
dialogue.lit: When I think to the road traveled since this first coffee together...

dialogue.t: —On était tellement jeunes et perdus, franchement.
dialogue.en: We were so young and lost, honestly.
dialogue.lit: One was so young and lost, frankly.

dialogue.t: —Et regarde-nous maintenant. Je suis tellement fier de nous tous.
dialogue.en: And look at us now. I'm so proud of all of us.
dialogue.lit: And look-us now. I am so proud of us all.

dialogue.t: —Marco, le sous-chef devenu propriétaire de son propre stand. Incroyable.
dialogue.en: Marco, the sous-chef turned owner of his own stand. Incredible.
dialogue.lit: Marco, the sous-chief become owner of his own stand. Incredible.

dialogue.t: —Léa, la chef d'équipe la plus respectée de son entreprise.
dialogue.en: Léa, the most respected team lead at her company.
dialogue.lit: Léa, the chief of-team the most respected of her company.

dialogue.t: —Et Camille, maman incroyable, et toujours notre meilleure amie.
dialogue.en: And Camille, an amazing mom, and still our best friend.
dialogue.lit: And Camille, mom incredible, and always our best friend.

dialogue.t: —On trinque à tout ça, alors. Au chemin parcouru, et à tout ce qui reste à venir.
dialogue.en: Let's toast to all that, then. To the road traveled, and to everything still to come.
dialogue.lit: One clinks to all that, then. To the road traveled, and to all that which remains to come.

dialogue.t: —Ça fait du bien, une matinée tranquille comme ça, tous ensemble.
dialogue.en: It feels good, a quiet morning like this, all together.
dialogue.lit: That makes of-the good, a morning quiet like that, all together.

dialogue.t: —Vraiment. Léo dort encore, on a un peu de temps pour nous.
dialogue.en: Really. Léo's still sleeping, we have a bit of time for ourselves.
dialogue.lit: Really. Léo sleeps still, one has a little of time for us.

dialogue.t: —Marco, tu veux un café ? J'en fais un pour tout le monde.
dialogue.en: Marco, do you want a coffee? I'm making one for everyone.
dialogue.lit: Marco, you want a coffee? I-of-it make one for all the world.

dialogue.t: —Avec plaisir, merci Hugo. Ça me rappelle nos débuts, ça.
dialogue.en: Gladly, thanks Hugo. That reminds me of our beginnings.
dialogue.lit: With pleasure, thanks Hugo. That me reminds our beginnings, that.

dialogue.t: —Le tout premier café qu'on a partagé ensemble, tu te souviens ?
dialogue.en: The very first coffee we shared together, do you remember?
dialogue.lit: The all first coffee that-one has shared together, you yourself remember?

dialogue.t: —Bien sûr, comment oublier ? Tu étais perdue, Léa, et tu cherchais ton chemin.
dialogue.en: Of course, how could I forget? You were lost, Léa, and looking for your way.
dialogue.lit: Well sure, how to-forget? You were lost, Léa, and you searched your way.

dialogue.t: —Et toi, Hugo, tu disais que t'étais pas d'ici, tu te souviens ?
dialogue.en: And you, Hugo, you said you weren't from here, remember?
dialogue.lit: And you, Hugo, you said that you-were not from-here, you yourself remember?

dialogue.t: —Ha ! C'est vrai. Et maintenant, je suis presque plus d'ici que n'importe qui.
dialogue.en: Ha! That's true. And now, I'm almost more from here than anyone.
dialogue.lit: Ha! It-is true. And now, I am almost more from-here than not-matters who.

dialogue.t: —On a fait du chemin, hein, depuis ce petit café d'il y a des années.
dialogue.en: We've come a long way, huh, since that little café years ago.
dialogue.lit: One has made of-the road, huh, since this little café of-it there has some years.

dialogue.t: —Énormément. Et c'est pas fini, j'ai l'impression.
dialogue.en: A huge amount. And it's not over, I feel like.
dialogue.lit: Enormously. And it-is not finished, I-have the impression.

dialogue.t: —On se balade un peu ? Comme avant, juste toi et moi.
dialogue.en: Shall we walk a bit? Like before, just you and me.
dialogue.lit: One oneself strolls a little? Like before, just you and me.

dialogue.t: —Avec plaisir, Léa. Ça me manquait, ces moments juste nous deux.
dialogue.en: Gladly, Léa. I missed these moments, just the two of us.
dialogue.lit: With pleasure, Léa. That me missed, these moments just us two.

dialogue.t: —Assieds-toi sur ce banc avec moi, j'ai envie d'être honnête avec toi.
dialogue.en: Sit on this bench with me, I want to be honest with you.
dialogue.lit: Sit-yourself on this bench with me, I-have desire of to-be honest with you.

dialogue.t: —D'accord. Qu'est-ce qu'il y a, Léa ?
dialogue.en: Okay. What's up, Léa?
dialogue.lit: Agreed. What-is-it there has, Léa?

dialogue.t: —Le lien qu'on a, toi et moi, il est tellement précieux pour moi. Tu le sais ?
dialogue.en: The bond we have, you and me, it's so precious to me. Do you know that?
dialogue.lit: The link that-one has, you and me, it is so precious for me. You it know?

dialogue.t: —Je le sais, et c'est pareil pour moi. T'es ma meilleure amie, vraiment.
dialogue.en: I know, and it's the same for me. You're my best friend, really.
dialogue.lit: I it know, and it-is same for me. You-are my best friend, really.

dialogue.t: —Quand t'es parti pour Lyon, j'ai eu peur de perdre ça, ce lien.
dialogue.en: When you left for Lyon, I was scared of losing that, this bond.
dialogue.lit: When you-are left for Lyon, I-have had fear of to-lose that, this link.

dialogue.t: —On l'a jamais perdu, Léa. La distance a rien changé entre nous.
dialogue.en: We never lost it, Léa. The distance changed nothing between us.
dialogue.lit: One it has never lost, Léa. The distance has nothing changed between us.

dialogue.t: —Je sais, maintenant. Merci d'être toujours revenu vers nous, vers moi.
dialogue.en: I know that now. Thanks for always coming back to us, to me.
dialogue.lit: I know, now. Thanks of to-be always come-back towards us, towards me.

dialogue.t: —Toujours, Léa. Ce banc, cette ville, vous... ça sera toujours chez moi.
dialogue.en: Always, Léa. This bench, this city, you all... it'll always be home.
dialogue.lit: Always, Léa. This bench, this city, you... that will-be always at me.

dialogue.t: —Maman ! Maman, viens vite, je crois que Léo va dire quelque chose !
dialogue.en: Mama! Mama, come quick, I think Léo's about to say something!
dialogue.lit: Mama! Mama, come quickly, I believe that Léo goes to-say something!

dialogue.t: —Quoi ?! J'arrive, j'arrive ! Léo, mon cœur, dis quelque chose !
dialogue.en: What?! I'm coming, I'm coming! Léo, sweetheart, say something!
dialogue.lit: What?! I-arrive, I-arrive! Léo, my heart, say something!

dialogue.t: —Allez, Léo, tu peux le faire. Essaie encore une fois.
dialogue.en: Come on, Léo, you can do it. Try one more time.
dialogue.lit: Go, Léo, you can it do. Try again one time.

dialogue.t: —Mar... co. Marco !
dialogue.en: Mar... co. Marco!
dialogue.lit: Mar... co. Marco!

dialogue.t: —Il a dit MON nom ?! Son premier mot, c'est MOI ?!
dialogue.en: He said MY name?! His first word is ME?!
dialogue.lit: He has said MY name?! His first word, it-is ME?!

dialogue.t: —Ha ha ha, évidemment ! C'est toujours toi qui lui donnes des crêpes !
dialogue.en: Ha ha ha, of course! You're always the one giving him crêpes!
dialogue.lit: Ha ha ha, evidently! It-is always you who to-him give some crêpes!

dialogue.t: —Je fonds complètement, là. C'est le plus beau jour de ma vie, presque.
dialogue.en: I'm completely melting right now. This is almost the best day of my life.
dialogue.lit: I melt completely, there. It-is the most beautiful day of my life, almost.

dialogue.t: —Léo, répète, dis 'Marco' encore une fois pour la vidéo !
dialogue.en: Léo, say it again, say 'Marco' one more time for the video!
dialogue.lit: Léo, repeat, say 'Marco' again one time for the video!

dialogue.t: —Marco ! Marco !
dialogue.en: Marco! Marco!
dialogue.lit: Marco! Marco!

dialogue.t: —On applaudit tous, allez ! Bravo, Léo, t'es vraiment adorable !
dialogue.en: Let's all clap, come on! Bravo, Léo, you're really adorable!
dialogue.lit: One applauds all, go! Bravo, Léo, you-are really adorable!

dialogue.t: —On devrait organiser une vraie grande fête, tous ensemble. Pour marquer le coup.
dialogue.en: We should throw a real big party, all together. To mark the occasion.
dialogue.lit: One should organize a great party, all together. For to-mark the blow.

dialogue.t: —J'adore l'idée. Mais marquer quel coup, exactement ?
dialogue.en: I love that idea. But mark what occasion, exactly?
dialogue.lit: I-adore the idea. But mark which blow, exactly?

dialogue.t: —Tout ! Le retour de Hugo, le succès de Marco, Léo qui grandit, tout ça.
dialogue.en: Everything! Hugo's return, Marco's success, Léo growing up, all of it.
dialogue.lit: All! The return of Hugo, the success of Marco, Léo who grows, all that.

dialogue.t: —On fait la liste des invités ensemble, alors. Toute notre famille, tous nos amis.
dialogue.en: Let's make the guest list together then. Our whole family, all our friends.
dialogue.lit: One makes the list of-the invited together, then. All our family, all our friends.

dialogue.t: —Marco, tu peux être le traiteur officiel ? Tes crêpes seraient parfaites.
dialogue.en: Marco, can you be the official caterer? Your crêpes would be perfect.
dialogue.lit: Marco, you can to-be the caterer official? Your crêpes would-be perfect.

dialogue.t: —Avec grand plaisir ! Je prépare déjà des idées de menu dans ma tête.
dialogue.en: With great pleasure! I'm already coming up with menu ideas in my head.
dialogue.lit: With great pleasure! I prepare already some ideas of menu in my head.

dialogue.t: —On décore le jardin de Camille, ça serait magnifique avec des lumières.
dialogue.en: We'll decorate Camille's garden, it would be gorgeous with lights.
dialogue.lit: One decorates the garden of Camille, that would-be magnificent with some lights.

dialogue.t: —J'adore. On invite vraiment tout le monde, même les voisins ?
dialogue.en: I love it. Do we really invite everyone, even the neighbors?
dialogue.lit: I-adore. One invites really all the world, even the neighbors?

dialogue.t: —Pourquoi pas ! Plus on est de fous, plus on rit.
dialogue.en: Why not! The more the merrier.
dialogue.lit: Why not! More one is of crazy-ones, more one laughs.

dialogue.t: —Alors c'est décidé. Samedi prochain, la plus grande fête qu'on ait jamais faite.
dialogue.en: So it's decided. Next Saturday, the biggest party we've ever thrown.
dialogue.lit: Then it-is decided. Saturday next, the biggest party that-one has ever made.

dialogue.t: —Regardez ce jardin, les guirlandes, la musique... c'est magnifique !
dialogue.en: Look at this garden, the lights, the music... it's beautiful!
dialogue.lit: Look this garden, the garlands, the music... it-is magnificent!

dialogue.t: —Marco a apporté plus de cent crêpes, je crois. Il a pas fait les choses à moitié.
dialogue.en: Marco brought more than a hundred crêpes, I think. He didn't do things by halves.
dialogue.lit: Marco has brought more than hundred crêpes, I believe. He has not made the things at half.

dialogue.t: —Évidemment ! C'est pas tous les jours qu'on fête tout ça d'un coup.
dialogue.en: Of course! It's not every day we celebrate all this at once.
dialogue.lit: Evidently! It-is not all the days that-one celebrates all that of-a blow.

dialogue.t: —Léo danse avec sa maman, regardez comme il rit aux éclats !
dialogue.en: Léo's dancing with his mom, look how he's laughing his head off!
dialogue.lit: Léo dances with his mom, look how he laughs at-the bursts!

dialogue.t: —Hugo, viens danser avec moi ! On a bien le droit de s'amuser un peu.
dialogue.en: Hugo, come dance with me! We're allowed to have a bit of fun.
dialogue.lit: Hugo, come to-dance with me! One has well the right of oneself to-amuse a little.

dialogue.t: —Avec plaisir, Léa ! Cette soirée est tellement parfaite.
dialogue.en: Gladly, Léa! This evening is so perfect.
dialogue.lit: With pleasure, Léa! This evening is so perfect.

dialogue.t: —Je veux porter un dernier toast avant que la nuit continue.
dialogue.en: I want to make one last toast before the night goes on.
dialogue.lit: I want to-carry a last toast before that the night continues.

dialogue.t: —Vas-y, Marco, on t'écoute tous.
dialogue.en: Go ahead, Marco, we're all listening.
dialogue.lit: Go-there, Marco, one you listens all.

dialogue.t: —À nous quatre, à Léo, à tout ce chemin parcouru ensemble. Au bonheur, simplement.
dialogue.en: To the four of us, to Léo, to all this road traveled together. To happiness, simply.
dialogue.lit: To us four, to Léo, to all this road traveled together. To the happiness, simply.

dialogue.t: —Au bonheur ! Et à encore mille autres soirées comme celle-ci.
dialogue.en: To happiness! And to a thousand more evenings like this one.
dialogue.lit: To the happiness! And to still thousand other evenings like this-one.

dialogue.t: —Vous savez quoi ? Ça fait exactement cent leçons qu'on se connaît, vous et moi.
dialogue.en: You know what? It's exactly a hundred lessons since we met, you and I.
dialogue.lit: You know what? That makes exactly hundred lessons that-one each-other knows, you and me.

dialogue.t: —Cent ?! Depuis ce premier salut au café, j'arrive pas à y croire.
dialogue.en: A hundred?! Since that first hello at the café, I can't believe it.
dialogue.lit: Hundred?! Since this first hi at-the café, I-arrive not to it to-believe.

dialogue.t: —On était de parfaits inconnus, et regardez-nous maintenant.
dialogue.en: We were complete strangers, and look at us now.
dialogue.lit: One was perfect strangers, and look-us now.

dialogue.t: —Une famille. C'est ça qu'on est devenus, vraiment, une vraie famille choisie.
dialogue.en: A family. That's what we've become, really, a true chosen family.
dialogue.lit: A family. It-is that which-one has become, really, a true family chosen.

dialogue.t: —Hugo qui est parti et revenu, Marco et son rêve réalisé, Léo qui grandit chaque jour.
dialogue.en: Hugo who left and came back, Marco and his realized dream, Léo growing every day.
dialogue.lit: Hugo who is left and come-back, Marco and his dream realized, Léo who grows each day.

dialogue.t: —Et toi, Léa, toujours là, depuis le tout premier jour, sans jamais bouger.
dialogue.en: And you, Léa, always here, since the very first day, never moving.
dialogue.lit: And you, Léa, always there, since the all first day, without ever to-move.

dialogue.t: —Parce que c'est ici, avec vous, que je voulais être. Depuis le début.
dialogue.en: Because it's here, with you, that I wanted to be. From the start.
dialogue.lit: Because it-is here, with you, that I wanted to-be. Since the beginning.

dialogue.t: —Merci pour tout, les amis. Vraiment, pour chaque moment, chaque rire, chaque larme.
dialogue.en: Thanks for everything, friends. Really, for every moment, every laugh, every tear.
dialogue.lit: Thanks for all, the friends. Really, for each moment, each laugh, each tear.

dialogue.t: —Merci à toi aussi. T'es resté avec nous, pour toujours, peu importe la distance.
dialogue.en: Thanks to you too. You stayed with us, forever, no matter the distance.
dialogue.lit: Thanks to you also. You-are stayed with us, for always, little matters the distance.

dialogue.t: —Alors, à la prochaine aventure, mes amis. La vraie vie, et tout ce qu'elle nous réserve.
dialogue.en: So, to the next adventure, my friends. Real life, and everything it has in store for us.
dialogue.lit: Then, to the next adventure, my friends. The real life, and all that-it us reserves.

dialogue.t: —On continue ensemble, comme toujours. Cent leçons plus tard, et c'est qu'un début.
dialogue.en: We carry on together, like always. A hundred lessons later, and it's only the beginning.
dialogue.lit: One continues together, like always. Hundred lessons more later, and it-is just a beginning.

dialogue.t: —Merci de nous avoir suivis jusqu'ici. À très bientôt, pour de nouvelles histoires.
dialogue.en: Thanks for following us this far. See you very soon, for new stories.
dialogue.lit: Thanks of us to-have followed until here. To very soon, for of-new stories.
